Event: Black Noir stabs Kimiko in the foot to pin her down
Explanation: He effectively restrains a dangerous opponent, benefiting himself while causing her pain.

Event: Black Noir cuts Kimiko’s torso open, disabling her briefly
Explanation: He severely injures her to neutralize her, gaining a major tactical edge while harming her.

Event: Kimiko kills everyone in the room when traffickers let her loose
Explanation: She destroys her captors (benefit to her) with extreme lethal force that also endangers / traumatizes others.

Event: Last captor shoots himself after seeing Kimiko escaped
Explanation: His suicide ends his own suffering while removing a dangerous criminal from the world.

Event: Kimiko escapes the lair and hunts down the woman who trafficked her
Explanation: She secures her freedom and revenge while brutally killing her former exploiter.

Event: Kimiko intervenes in the alley and saves Frenchie from Black Noir
Explanation: She risks herself to stop Noir and preserve a vital ally and loved one.

Event: Kimiko hugs Annie and holds hands in gratitude when they reunite
Explanation: She strengthens a helpful alliance and expresses thanks, improving trust for both.

Event: Black Noir apparently kills Kimiko in the alley (before healing kicks in)
Explanation: He believes he has removed a major threat, a gain for him at substantial harm to her.

Event: Kimiko holds off Black Noir and gets gutted to keep him from Frenchie
Explanation: She incurs serious injury to delay Noir and protect Frenchie, gaining little herself.

Event: Kimiko later breaks free and slaughters Nina’s gang of criminals
Explanation: She eliminates those who captured and threatened her, benefiting herself via savage retribution.

Event: Nina runs away in fear after Kimiko attacks her gang
Explanation: Her retreat preserves her own life and ends the fight without adding harm to bystanders.

Event: A-Train smashes Kimiko’s head at super speed, trying to kill her
Explanation: He attempts a lethal removal of a perceived obstacle, believing it secures him at her extreme expense.

Event: Stormfront snaps Kimiko’s neck in battle
Explanation: She tries to eliminate an enemy, which would aid her side while gravely harming Kimiko.

Event: Kimiko re-snaps her neck into place and returns to the fight
Explanation: She rapidly restores herself, regaining combat advantage to continue brutal attacks.

Event: Kimiko regrows limbs and fully heals from extreme injuries over time
Explanation: Her use of regeneration preserves her effectiveness and reassures allies depending on her.

Event: Tala ambushes Kimiko with a point-blank chest shot
Explanation: She gains a short-lived edge by devastating Kimiko’s body, though the advantage quickly erodes.

Event: Kimiko pieces herself back together and returns to fight Tala
Explanation: She leverages her healing to re-enter combat and overpower an enemy.

Event: Kimiko breaks A-Train’s leg with a pipe as timed backup for Hughie
Explanation: She executes a precise disabling strike that protects Hughie and advances the team’s mission.

Event: Kimiko invents a new sign language with her brother
Explanation: They create a communication system that empowers both of them and improves coordination.

Event: Kimiko teaches Frenchie to understand her signs
Explanation: Sharing her language deepens mutual understanding and makes them more effective partners.

Event: Kimiko drinks many beers to cope after seeing a misused trafficking photo
Explanation: She self-medicates with alcohol that her healing quickly cancels, gaining little and risking impairment.

Event: Kimiko gets into a scuffle with the conspiracy theorist who misused the image
Explanation: She vents anger through violence that punishes a harmful actor but mainly serves her outrage.

Event: Kimiko becomes a professional killer after Kenji’s death
Explanation: She profits and channels rage via murder-for-hire, benefitting herself while harming victims.

Event: Frenchie lets Kimiko out of her cage in the traffickers’ lair
Explanation: He frees a dangerous captive who becomes a powerful ally and later saves his life.

Event: Kimiko immediately starts slaughtering the gangsters after release
Explanation: She secures her escape and vengeance by massacring her captors.

Event: Kimiko snaps the neck of a Shining Light member she recognizes
Explanation: She takes revenge on a perpetrator of her parents’ murder, helping herself and society via brutal means.

Event: Kimiko nearly rips that Shining Light member’s head off afterward
Explanation: She indulges in excessive post-mortem violence, slightly relieving her trauma while adding gratuitous harm.

Event: Stormfront kills Kenji in front of Kimiko
Explanation: She removes a target/enemy, a gain for her side, at devastating cost to Kimiko and Kenji.

Event: Kimiko goes on a Roaring Rampage of Revenge against Stormfront later
Explanation: She exacts vengeance on her brother’s killer, emotionally benefiting herself while inflicting justified harm on a villain.

Event: Kimiko tells Billy via text “I’m not your fucking gun”
Explanation: She asserts autonomy and resists exploitative use, improving her self-respect and drawing a necessary boundary.

Event: Butcher insists “That is exactly what you are”
Explanation: He reinforces her objectification to preserve a useful asset, helping himself at her emotional expense.

Event: Kimiko decides to ask Annie for Compound V to get her powers back
Explanation: She chooses to re-arm herself despite trauma in order to better protect Frenchie and the team.

Event: Kimiko asks Annie to acquire Compound V for her
Explanation: She enlists a trusted ally in a risky plan that increases her power and the group’s safety.

Event: Soldier Boy accidentally depowers Kimiko
Explanation: He removes a major threat but also eliminates a potential ally/asset and destabilizes the balance with no clear net gain.

Event: Kimiko rejoices at losing her powers as a curse lifted
Explanation: Seeing the depowering as liberation improves her mental health and eases her relationship with others.

Event: Black bag operatives tranquilize Kimiko and keep her sedated
Explanation: They effectively control a dangerous Supe, aiding their mission while severely disempowering her.

Event: Kimiko survives brutal battles against Nina’s enforcers even while depowered
Explanation: She proves her resilience and combat skill, eliminating threats while taking some damage herself.

Event: Kimiko disobeys Butcher and comes into conflict with him
Explanation: Resisting his abusive orders protects her autonomy and arguably reduces his worst excesses.

Event: Frenchie apologizes to Kimiko for forcing her into being his emotional crutch
Explanation: His apology improves their emotional health and mutual trust.

Event: Kimiko and Frenchie choose to live together after reconciling
Explanation: Co-habitation strengthens their support system and operational teamwork.

Event: Kimiko pushes Frenchie toward a relationship with Colin
Explanation: She sacrifices her own desires to prioritize his happiness, undermining her own romantic wishes.

Event: Kimiko later frankly tells Frenchie she loves him but says they can’t be together
Explanation: She is honest yet cautious, trying to protect both of them from a relationship she believes would be dangerous.

Event: Kimiko encourages Frenchie to be with Colin and be happy
Explanation: She again subordinates her feelings to his perceived well-being, helping him while hurting herself.

Event: Kimiko and Frenchie finally admit mutual feelings and become a couple
Explanation: Their union strengthens both their emotional lives and their mutual support in the team.

Event: Kimiko becomes more cheerful and kind over time toward the group
Explanation: Her emotional growth benefits her mental state and solidifies team cohesion.

Event: Kimiko often goes out of her way to make enemies’ deaths as painful as possible
Explanation: She indulges in cruel revenge that satisfies her but inflicts extra suffering beyond what’s tactically needed.

Event: Kimiko cares deeply for friends and innocents and protects them
Explanation: Her protective stance preserves others’ lives and gives her purpose and bonds.

Event: Kimiko tells The Worm his voice makes her want to kill herself
Explanation: Her brutal comment offers no benefit beyond momentary venting while needlessly hurting and alienating him.

Event: Kimiko suggests killing Butcher to prevent the virus from spreading
Explanation: Given the danger, killing him is a hard utilitarian proposal to protect the wider world at some moral and emotional cost.

Event: Kimiko confesses she thought Frenchie deserved better and pushed him away
Explanation: Her insecurity hurts her own romantic prospects while being framed as for his benefit.

Event: Kimiko texts that she needs to face her past “So I’m going to kill them all”
Explanation: She confronts her trauma via lethal revenge, aiding her catharsis while inflicting retributive harm.

Event: Kimiko downs several beers in the middle of a mission to cope with a trigger
Explanation: She impairs herself during an op, endangering both her own performance and team safety.

Event: MM keeps Kimiko calm when she’s triggered by Stormfront’s presence
Explanation: He reduces her risk of losing control, helping her emotional state and team safety.

Event: Kimiko hallucinates/imagines herself singing and dancing in musicals
Explanation: Her fantasies serve as a harmless coping mechanism that improves her mood.

Event: Kimiko kisses Frenchie in hospital after fantasizing about singing with him
Explanation: Acting on mutual attraction deepens their bond and emotional connection.

Event: Kimiko laughs then beats down Stormfront in the finale fight
Explanation: She savors and executes brutal revenge against a major enemy, personally satisfying but centered on harm.

Event: Kimiko screams “NO!” and regains her voice when Frenchie is taken away
Explanation: Her anguish unlocks speech, aiding her own recovery and signaling her devotion to him.

Event: Kimiko undergoes intensive speech therapy to overcome mutism
Explanation: Hard therapeutic work dramatically improves her functioning and relationships.

Event: Kimiko learns to speak partly by watching TikTok and becomes a Motor Mouth
Explanation: Her unorthodox practice boosts her fluency and lets her connect, despite occasional social roughness.
